title: Mental Health Counselor

rationales.taskAutomation: Session-note drafting, treatment-plan templates and screening-assessment scoring are automatable; the therapeutic relationship itself is not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI scribes and screening tools for behavioral health are credible and improving, but nothing role-specific yet matches the maturity of general clinical documentation tools.
rationales.adoption: Tech-forward practices and telehealth platforms use AI note-taking and intake tools; adoption elsewhere remains early.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can own intake scheduling, screening-tool scoring and note drafting, but crisis intervention and the counseling relationship stay squarely human.

salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 21-1014 (Mental Health Counselors), p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI now drafts session notes and scores standard screening tools, cutting real administrative load. What it can't do is sit with a person in crisis, and demand for that keeps outpacing supply.
